steps.title: Test the universal claim
steps.content: The student claims ego-based time concepts are universal, meaning temporal gestures should depend on the speaker's own orientation, such as left, right, front, or back. A weakening finding must show a group using a reference frame that stays fixed even when the speaker faces a different direction.

steps.title: Reject non-weakening findings
steps.content: Choice A could still fit a body-relative system because opposite directions might mean front and back. Choice B concerns grammar, not spatial reference frames. Choice D compares gesture frequency, but using fewer gestures would not show whether the remaining gestures are ego-based or landscape-based. That distinction is what weakens universality. The answer is C.
